plant.description: Unique History: Mentioned in ancient Ayurvedic treatises like Charaka Samhita as a potent 'Sangrahi' pulse. Historically used during droughts as a primary protein source due to its extreme resilience. Regarded as 'Ancient Mother of Endurance'. Physical Characteristics: A small, erect, annual herb reaching up to 0.3-0.6 meters in height, with trifoliate leaves and small, white-to-violet flowers. The fruit consists of small follicles (pods) containing numerous flat, brownish-black-to-red seeds. Found throughout India and tropical regions worldwide, cultivated extensively for its seeds. Specific Chemical Compounds: Contains Siringic acid, Ferulic acid, and diversos Protease inhibitors. The presence of phenolic acids is crucial for its neuro-relaxant, anti-tumor, and anti-inflammatory action on the heart and skin cells. Strict Standardizations: The seed is standardized in API based on its greyish-white-to-brown color and characteristic sweet-mild-nutty taste. Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<8%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>15%).

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Powerful pacifier of Pitta and Kapha doshas. Due to its Madhura (sweet) and Kashaya (astringent) Rasa and Sheeta (cooling) Virya, it is the most prestigious legume for 'Sangrahi' (absorbent) and 'Meda-hara' (fat-relieving) therapies. While it is cooling, it is 'Ruksha' (dry) and 'Laghu' (light), making it highly Vata-increasing (Vatala) if not detoxified and consumed excessively.

detoxification_process.method: MUST be soaked overnight and the water discarded before boiling. Boil three times and discard water to reduce antinutrients.

yusha__medicated_soup_.method: 1 part detoxified seeds boiled with 14 parts water, seasoned with ginger and cumin. Take for managing diarrhea and weakness.

triputi_odana__cooked_rice_.method: Detoxified seeds cooked with 4 parts water and seasoned with digestive spices like pepper and hing to counter the Vata-increasing effect.

external_paste__lepa_.method: Ground fresh seeds or powder applied directly to localized inflammations (Mumps, boils, skin rashes, hyperpigmentation) to reduce heat and swelling.

dosage.children: Use with caution. 5 - 10 grams of detoxified cooked pulse mixed with ghee once twice for muscle development.
dosage.adults: 30-50g of detoxified cooked pulse per meal, limited to twice weekly.
dosage.elderly: Highly recommended to prevent age-related Pitta issues and maintain skin radiance. Dosage: 30g of cooked pulse once weekly.

medicinal_uses: Expert Internal Weight Management and Fat-Scraping Specialist (Medahara): Triputi is the premier Ayurvedic pulse for clearing stubborn abdominal fat. Its 'Ruksha' (dry) and 'Grahi' (absorbent) nature helps in absorbing systemic 'Meda' (fat) and providing systemic stabilization of the 'Kapha' channels. WHO-aligned research confirms its high digestibility once the ODAP neurotoxin is reduced through soaking and boiling.

Gastrointestinal and Diarrhea Care Specialist (Sangrahi): Historically used for managing burning diarrhea and dysentery. Its 'Grahi' (absorbent) and 'Kashaya' (astringent) actions help in strengthening the gut lining and binding the stool without causing heaviness.

Internal Hemorrhage and Bleeding Specialist (Raktapitta): Recognized as 'Raktapitta-hara' (bleeding-destroyer). It effectively cools the blood and stops internal bleeding from the lungs, stomach, and pelvic organs by providing systemic cooling and providing systemic hemostatic balance.

Anti-inflammatory and Fever Relief: Its cooling potency (Sheeta) provides instant relief from internal burning sensations (Daha), sunstrokes, and inflammatory fevers. It helps in Reducing systemic heat and inflammatory congestion through its systemic 'Raktaprasadana' (blood-soothing) effects.
Urinary and Kidney Support: As a mild diuretic, it helps in clearing urinary stagnations and managing infections by cooling the Pitta in the bladder and kidneys.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Proteins (25-28%), Carbohydrates, Flavonoids, Polyphenols, and Essential Minerals (Phosphorus, Iron). Contains specific amino acids (Homoarginine) and an antinutrient (ODAP) which must be removed by soaking and boiling.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: Classified as a medicinal pulse requiring professional guidance for therapeutic use.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: NOT for daily consumption in large quantities without detoxification. Excessive use of non-detoxified seeds leads to 'Lathyrism' (Pangu-roga) - a neurological condition causing lower limb paralysis. Avoid in high Vata, pregnancy, and chronic kidney disease.
possible_side_effects: Overconsumption or improper cooking leads to nerve weakness and stiffness in the legs.
drug_interactions: None documented. It acts as a neutral carrier for many medicines.

plant_profile.note: AI estimated profile based on known properties
